title: dryopteris subsimilis
intro: Dryopteris subsimilis, also known as the subsimilar wood fern, is a species of fern that is native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dryopteris subsimilis include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its ability to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Dryopteris subsimilis plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
